notes: Character model doesn't show up, extremely low FPS once enemies appear
title: Hyperdimension Neptunia U: Action Unleashed

notes: Character is invisible, and sometimes monsters, too. Shadows are still visible though, as is the character's weapon, so the game *technically* is playable. Great framerate and DualShock 4 support.
title: Hyperdimension Neptunia U: Action Unleashed

notes:
Common: the videos do not load but can be skipped.
i965: great performance (seems better than native) but has tiny graphical bugs: the mini-map blinks rarely and some visual effects play very slowly while paused. Perfectly playable.
iris: suffers a very significant performance hit (75% FPS loss) and has the same tiny graphical bugs but very rarely, maybe because FPS is lower. Iris demands more memory bandwidth, which seems to be the cause for this performance difference.
title: Hyperdimension Neptunia U: Action Unleashed

notes:
The videos at the start don't load but can be skipped.
The game looks as it should but the frame rate is really low when enemies are on the screen.
title: Hyperdimension Neptunia U: Action Unleashed

notes: A couple of the screens are black when loading the game, but the game itself works. The game does slow down when monsters spawn though.
title: Hyperdimension Neptunia U: Action Unleashed

notes:
The game runs, but the intro is black screen that you skip to get to main menu. No windows media player tweaks in 32bit prefix had changed that. Gameplay works as it should except for its slowdowns when too many monsters. Though I dont notice it with my rig but after a while it slows. 
Other than that, game runs like I would want it to.
title: Hyperdimension Neptunia U: Action Unleashed
